# M6: CIP-0164 multi-node topology migration

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Flip the phase-2 welfare suites from `topology-single-producer.yaml` onto a CIP-0164 Table 7-aligned 600-pool topology, add a fork-resolution metric so multi-node noise is visible, regenerate goldens, and re-measure welfare honestly.

**Architecture:** YAML-first. Authors a new 600-pool topology generated by a one-shot Python script (Pareto α≈1.4 stakes, 4-region geography, mesh peer graph). Calibrates `vote-probability = 600` and `vote-threshold = 450` (75 % quorum of n=600 expected votes — math verified, no Rust changes needed for stake-weighting; the existing `vrf_probabilities × compute_target_vrf_stake` pipeline is correct). Adds one new `Event::LinearPricingSampleApplied` emitted before each `apply_priced_block` call so the collector can count slot-battle sibling RBs and orphaned pricing samples at the representative node. Goldens regen + verify after the user runs the full sweep externally.

**Tech Stack:** Rust (sim-core, sim-cli), YAML configs, Python (one-shot topology generator), bash (parallel runner).

---

## Compute split

I (Claude) implement code + configs + smoke tests. **User runs the full 16-suite sweep externally** with `sim-rs/scripts/run-parallel-suites.sh 10` after I hand off the command. I resume on the resulting output dirs for goldens regen, variance analysis, and write-up.

## Key calibration values (decided up front)

- `vote-probability: 600` (was 500 under single-producer). Calibration: with stake-weighted Poisson per `lottery.rs`, each node's expected hits = `share × vote_probability`; total expected hits across all 600 pools = `vote_probability = 600` ≈ CIP-0164 n.
- `vote-threshold: 450` (= 75 % of 600).
- `rb-generation-probability: 0.05` (unchanged).
- 600 pools, Pareto α=1.4 stakes, sum total ≈ 3 × 10^10 (mainnet-aligned with headroom).
- 4 regions: NA-East 30 %, EU 30 %, NA-West 20 %, Asia 20 %. Intra 25 ms ± 5 ms, inter 80–150 ms by region pair.
- 20 peers/node, 70 % intra-region, 30 % inter-region.
- `cpu-core-count: 4` everywhere.
- One pool (the largest, deterministically chosen) carries `tx-generation-weight: 1`; the rest are validators.
- VRF stake quantization: with Pareto α=1.4 over 600 pools summing to 3 × 10^10, the smallest pool's stake is `≫ 2000` → `target_vrf_stake = stake × 0.05 ≫ 100` → no truncation-to-zero. **Verified analytically; no minimum-floor clamping needed.**

---

## File structure

| Path | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| `sim-rs/scripts/generate-cip-topology.py` (new) | One-shot generator. Pareto stakes, 4-region geography, mesh peers, dumps YAML. Deterministic seed pinned. Committed alongside its output. |
| `sim-rs/parameters/phase-2-sweep/topology-cip-realistic.yaml` (new) | 600-pool topology output by the generator. Committed verbatim. |
| `sim-rs/parameters/phase-2-sweep/topology-single-producer.yaml` (modify header) | Updated header: "kernel-correctness reference; no longer the welfare default". File itself unchanged. |
| `sim-rs/parameters/phase-2-sweep/protocol-base.yaml` (modify) | `vote-probability: 600`, `vote-threshold: 450`. |
| `sim-rs/parameters/phase-2-sweep/protocol-rb-reduced-{half,third,quarter}.yaml` (modify) | Propagate the same two values. |
| `sim-rs/parameters/phase-2-sweep/suites/*.yaml` (modify all 16) | `default-topology: parameters/phase-2-sweep/topology-cip-realistic.yaml`. |
| `sim-rs/sim-core/src/events.rs` (modify) | New `Event::LinearPricingSampleApplied { node, slot, producer }`. `EventTracker::track_linear_pricing_sample_applied(...)`. |
| `sim-rs/sim-core/src/sim/linear_leios.rs` (modify ~1 line + emit) | Emit the new event right before `self.apply_priced_block(&rb);` inside `publish_rb`. |
| `sim-rs/sim-cli/src/metrics/collector.rs` (modify) | Two new `RunSummary` fields (`slot_battles_count`, `orphaned_pricing_samples`). New `HashMap<u64, HashSet<NodeId>>` accumulator in collector; `ingest` matches the new event; `finalise` reduces to the two counters. |
| `sim-rs/sim-cli/src/metrics/comparison.rs` (modify) | Surface the two new fields in `metrics_comparison.txt` and the run-only writer. |
| `sim-rs/sim-cli/src/metrics/diagnostics.rs` (modify) | Surface in `diagnostics.log`. |
| `sim-rs/sim-cli/tests/determinism.rs` | No structural change. Goldens at `parameters/phase-2-sweep/suites/.goldens/*.sha256` flip on regen. |
| `sim-rs/scripts/run-parallel-suites.sh` (modify defaults) | Default to all 16 suites; document the multi-node runtime expectation. |
| `docs/phase-2/m6-handoff.md` (new) | Slot-battle decision rationale; measured noise bound; key deltas vs single-producer. |
| `docs/phase-2/multi-node-results.md` (new) | Side-by-side welfare table: 5 pricing configs × 4 demands × {single-producer, multi-node}. Honest discussion of inversions. |
| `CLAUDE.md` (modify) | Update suite count (7 → 16), topology section, vote-probability/threshold rationale. |

## Phase A — Code & config (my work)

### Task A1: Topology generator + topology-cip-realistic.yaml

**Files:**
- Create: `sim-rs/scripts/generate-cip-topology.py`
- Create: `sim-rs/parameters/phase-2-sweep/topology-cip-realistic.yaml`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the generator**

`sim-rs/scripts/generate-cip-topology.py`:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
CIP-0164 Table 7-aligned phase-2 topology generator (one-shot).

Output: parameters/phase-2-sweep/topology-cip-realistic.yaml

Properties (CIP-0164 Table 7 + Cardano mainnet realism):
  - 600 stake pools (matches mean committee n = 600).
  - Pareto(alpha=1.4) stake distribution, total ~= 3e10 (mainnet ~22B ADA + headroom).
  - 4 regions: NA-East 30%, EU 30%, NA-West 20%, Asia 20%.
  - 25 ms +/- 5 ms intra-region; 80-150 ms inter-region by region pair.
  - 20 peers/node, 70% intra-region 30% inter-region.
  - cpu-core-count: 4 everywhere; one pool (largest stake) carries tx-generation-weight: 1.
  - All bandwidth = 1,024,000 B/s (matches existing topology.default.yaml).

Run from sim-rs/:
  python3 scripts/generate-cip-topology.py > parameters/phase-2-sweep/topology-cip-realistic.yaml

The generator is deterministic (seeded). The output YAML is committed verbatim;
the generator is committed alongside as the source of truth.
"""

import random
import sys
import yaml

SEED = 0xCIP0164  # noqa: E741  intentional
N_POOLS = 600
PARETO_ALPHA = 1.4
TOTAL_STAKE = 30_000_000_000  # 3e10
PEERS_PER_NODE = 20
INTRA_REGION_PEER_FRACTION = 0.70
BANDWIDTH_BPS = 1_024_000

REGIONS = [
    ("na-east", 0.30, (-75.0, 40.0)),  # name, share, (lon, lat)
    ("eu",      0.30, ( 10.0, 50.0)),
    ("na-west", 0.20, (-120.0, 37.0)),
    ("asia",    0.20, (140.0, 35.0)),
]

INTRA_REGION_MS_MEAN = 25.0
INTRA_REGION_MS_STD = 5.0
INTER_REGION_MS = {
    ("na-east", "eu"):      90.0,
    ("na-east", "na-west"): 70.0,
    ("na-east", "asia"):    150.0,
    ("eu",      "na-west"): 130.0,
    ("eu",      "asia"):    150.0,
    ("na-west", "asia"):    110.0,
}


def inter_region_latency(a, b, rng):
    if a == b:
        return max(1.0, rng.gauss(INTRA_REGION_MS_MEAN, INTRA_REGION_MS_STD))
    key = tuple(sorted([a, b]))
    base = INTER_REGION_MS.get(key) or INTER_REGION_MS[(key[1], key[0])]
    return max(1.0, rng.gauss(base, 15.0))


def main():
    rng = random.Random(SEED)

    # 1. Stakes: Pareto draws, scaled to TOTAL_STAKE.
    raw = [rng.paretovariate(PARETO_ALPHA) for _ in range(N_POOLS)]
    raw_sum = sum(raw)
    stakes = sorted(
        (int(round(r * TOTAL_STAKE / raw_sum)) for r in raw),
        reverse=True,
    )
    # Pin total exactly: dump residual onto the smallest pool.
    delta = TOTAL_STAKE - sum(stakes)
    stakes[-1] += delta
    assert sum(stakes) == TOTAL_STAKE
    # Smallest pool sanity check: stake * 0.05 >= 100 (no VRF-truncation-to-0).
    assert stakes[-1] * 0.05 >= 100, f"smallest stake too small: {stakes[-1]}"

    # 2. Region assignment.
    region_names = []
    target = {r[0]: int(round(r[1] * N_POOLS)) for r in REGIONS}
    while sum(target.values()) < N_POOLS:
        target[REGIONS[0][0]] += 1
    for name, count in target.items():
        region_names.extend([name] * count)
    rng.shuffle(region_names)

    # 3. Per-node locations (jittered around region centroid in lon/lat).
    region_centroids = {r[0]: r[2] for r in REGIONS}
    node_records = []
    for i in range(N_POOLS):
        region = region_names[i]
        lon, lat = region_centroids[region]
        node_records.append({
            "name": f"pool-{i:03d}",
            "stake": stakes[i],
            "region": region,
            "location": [lon + rng.gauss(0, 5.0), lat + rng.gauss(0, 5.0)],
        })

    # 4. Peer graph: 20 peers/node, 70% intra-region, 30% inter-region.
    intra_count = int(round(PEERS_PER_NODE * INTRA_REGION_PEER_FRACTION))
    inter_count = PEERS_PER_NODE - intra_count
    by_region = {r[0]: [n["name"] for n in node_records if n["region"] == r[0]] for r in REGIONS}
    region_lookup = {n["name"]: n["region"] for n in node_records}

    nodes_out = {}
    for n in node_records:
        same = [p for p in by_region[n["region"]] if p != n["name"]]
        other = [p for p in (sum(by_region.values(), [])) if region_lookup[p] != n["region"]]
        peers = rng.sample(same, min(intra_count, len(same))) + \
                rng.sample(other, min(inter_count, len(other)))
        producers = {}
        for peer in peers:
            producers[peer] = {
                "bandwidth-bytes-per-second": BANDWIDTH_BPS,
                "latency-ms": round(inter_region_latency(n["region"], region_lookup[peer], rng), 3),
            }
        entry = {
            "stake": n["stake"],
            "cpu-core-count": 4,
            "location": n["location"],
            "producers": producers,
        }
        nodes_out[n["name"]] = entry

    # 5. Single tx-generation source: largest stake pool.
    tx_source = node_records[0]["name"]  # node_records is sorted-by-stake desc
    nodes_out[tx_source]["tx-generation-weight"] = 1

    header = (
        "# CIP-0164 Table 7-aligned phase-2 topology (600 pools).\n"
        "#\n"
        "# Generated by sim-rs/scripts/generate-cip-topology.py (seed pinned).\n"
        "# Total stake 3 x 10^10 (mainnet ~22B ADA + headroom). Pareto(alpha=1.4)\n"
        "# stake distribution. 4 regions (NA-East 30%, EU 30%, NA-West 20%,\n"
        "# Asia 20%). 20 peers/node, 70% intra-region. cpu-core-count: 4.\n"
        "# Single tx-generation source = pool-000 (largest stake).\n"
        "#\n"
        "# vote-probability calibration (set in protocol-base.yaml): 600.\n"
        "# vote-threshold: 450 (75 percent of n = 600).\n"
        "# Smallest pool stake x rb-generation-probability (0.05) >> 100,\n"
        "# so no target_vrf_stake -> 0 truncation.\n"
    )
    sys.stdout.write(header)
    yaml.safe_dump({"nodes": nodes_out}, sys.stdout, sort_keys=True, default_flow_style=False)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the generator**

```bash
cd sim-rs
python3 scripts/generate-cip-topology.py > parameters/phase-2-sweep/topology-cip-realistic.yaml
wc -l parameters/phase-2-sweep/topology-cip-realistic.yaml
```

Expected: ~14,500 lines (600 nodes × ~24 lines each).

- [ ] **Step 3: Sanity-check the output**

```bash
cd sim-rs
python3 -c "
import yaml
with open('parameters/phase-2-sweep/topology-cip-realistic.yaml') as f:
    t = yaml.safe_load(f)
nodes = t['nodes']
print('node count:', len(nodes))
stakes = [n['stake'] for n in nodes.values()]
print('total stake:', sum(stakes))
print('smallest stake:', min(stakes))
print('smallest * 0.05:', min(stakes) * 0.05)
tx_sources = [n for n, v in nodes.items() if v.get('tx-generation-weight')]
print('tx-source nodes:', tx_sources)
peer_counts = [len(v['producers']) for v in nodes.values()]
print('mean peers:', sum(peer_counts)/len(peer_counts))
"
```

Expected:
- `node count: 600`
- `total stake: 30000000000`
- `smallest * 0.05` ≥ 100
- `tx-source nodes: ['pool-000']`
- `mean peers: 20.0`

### Task A2: Update single-producer topology header

**Files:**
- Modify: `sim-rs/parameters/phase-2-sweep/topology-single-producer.yaml` (lines 1-14)

- [ ] **Step 1: Rewrite the header**

Replace lines 1-14 with:

```yaml
# Single-producer topology — kernel-correctness reference ONLY.
#
# Retained for unit/integration tests and as a controlled regime
# where slot battles cannot occur. NOT the welfare default — the
# phase-2 welfare suites have moved to topology-cip-realistic.yaml
# (CIP-0164 Table 7 calibrated, 600 pools, mainnet-like). See
# docs/phase-2/m6-handoff.md.
#
# Stake granularity: at low rb-generation-probability values (e.g.,
# Cardano-realistic 0.05) the lottery's `target_vrf_stake` is
# `stake × probability` (see lottery.rs::compute_target_vrf_stake).
# With stake=1 and prob=0.05 that truncates to 0 and the producer
# never wins. Use stake=100000 so the truncation preserves the
# probability (100000 × 0.05 = 5000, win iff random_range(0..100000)
# < 5000, i.e. exactly 5 percent per slot).
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Commit**

```bash
git add sim-rs/parameters/phase-2-sweep/topology-single-producer.yaml
git commit -m "M6: mark single-producer topology as kernel-correctness reference"
```

- [ ] **Step 1: Update protocol-base.yaml**

Replace the `vote-threshold: 1` block (around line 66) with:

```yaml
# CIP-0164 Table 7 calibrated multi-node voting.
#
# Pre-M6 (single-producer): vote-probability=500, vote-threshold=1.
# One node owned all stake, so per-RB it ran 500 always-win lotteries
# and its single vote certified the EB trivially.
#
# Post-M6: target n = 600 expected votes per RB across 600 stake-weighted
# pools. Stake-weighted Poisson per lottery.rs::vrf_probabilities:
# Sum_i (stake_i / total_stake) × vote_probability = vote_probability ≈ 600.
# Threshold = 75 % of n = 450.
vote-probability: 600
vote-threshold: 450
```

(If `vote-probability` is currently set via `config.default.yaml` overlay, also add it here explicitly. Confirm by `grep -rn vote-probability sim-rs/parameters/ | grep -v sweep` before this step.)

For each of `protocol-rb-reduced-{half,third,quarter}.yaml`, replace the existing `vote-threshold: 1` line with the same two-line block (preserving the local rb-body-max-size-bytes override).

### Task A4: Fork-resolution metric (TDD)

**REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** superpowers:test-driven-development.

**Files:**
- Modify: `sim-rs/sim-core/src/events.rs` (new event variant + tracker method)
- Modify: `sim-rs/sim-core/src/sim/linear_leios.rs` (emit before `apply_priced_block` in `publish_rb`)
- Modify: `sim-rs/sim-cli/src/metrics/collector.rs` (new `RunSummary` fields + accumulator + ingest)
- Modify: `sim-rs/sim-cli/src/metrics/comparison.rs` + `diagnostics.rs` (surface)

**Metric semantics** (decided up-front, no TBD):
- `slot_battles_count: u64` — number of distinct slots at which the representative node validated ≥ 2 RBs (sibling RBs from different producers at the same slot).
- `orphaned_pricing_samples: u64` — sum over slot-battle slots of (N_RBs_at_slot − 1). One sample per sibling RB; the representative's chain-selection picks exactly one as canonical, so the rest are orphans.

Both derived from a single new event emitted once per `apply_priced_block` invocation.

- [ ] **Step 1: Locate the existing `Event` enum + `EventTracker` patterns**

```bash
cd sim-rs
grep -n 'pub enum Event' sim-core/src/events.rs
grep -n 'track_linear_rb_received' sim-core/src/events.rs
```

Read the surrounding 30 lines for both. The new event mirrors the shape of `Event::RBReceived` (carries `node`, `slot`); the tracker method mirrors `track_linear_rb_received`.

- [ ] **Step 2: Write the failing collector test**

Add to `sim-rs/sim-cli/src/metrics/collector.rs` (in the `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block):

```rust
#[test]
fn slot_battle_metric_counts_sibling_rbs_at_representative() {
    let mut c = MetricsCollector::new(0.05);
    c.set_representative_node("pool-000");

    // Slot 10: two sibling RBs at the representative (pool-A and pool-B).
    c.ingest(&Event::LinearPricingSampleApplied {
        node: "pool-000".into(),
        slot: 10,
        producer: "pool-A".into(),
    });
    c.ingest(&Event::LinearPricingSampleApplied {
        node: "pool-000".into(),
        slot: 10,
        producer: "pool-B".into(),
    });
    // Slot 11: a single RB — no battle.
    c.ingest(&Event::LinearPricingSampleApplied {
        node: "pool-000".into(),
        slot: 11,
        producer: "pool-A".into(),
    });
    // Slot 20: three siblings — counts as 1 battle, 2 orphans.
    for p in ["pool-A", "pool-B", "pool-C"] {
        c.ingest(&Event::LinearPricingSampleApplied {
            node: "pool-000".into(),
            slot: 20,
            producer: p.into(),
        });
    }
    // Non-representative node: ignored.
    c.ingest(&Event::LinearPricingSampleApplied {
        node: "pool-099".into(),
        slot: 10,
        producer: "pool-Z".into(),
    });

    let (_, summary) = c.finalise();
    assert_eq!(summary.slot_battles_count, 2, "slots 10 and 20");
    assert_eq!(summary.orphaned_pricing_samples, 3, "slot 10: 1 + slot 20: 2");
}
```

```bash
cd sim-rs
cargo test --package sim-cli --lib slot_battle_metric -- --nocapture 2>&1 | head -40
```

Expected: compile error `no variant or associated item named LinearPricingSampleApplied found for enum Event`.

- [ ] **Step 4: Add the event variant**

In `sim-rs/sim-core/src/events.rs`, find the `pub enum Event { ... }` declaration. Add (next to `RBReceived`):

```rust
/// Emitted once per `apply_priced_block` invocation. The
/// representative-node collector uses this to count slot-battle
/// sibling RBs and orphaned pricing samples (M6 fork-resolution
/// metric — see docs/phase-2/m6-handoff.md).
LinearPricingSampleApplied {
    node: NodeId,
    slot: u64,
    producer: NodeId,
},
```

(`NodeId` shape: check existing variants — `RBReceived` uses `NodeId` already. Match its conventions exactly — `String` newtype vs raw `String`, etc.)

Also add the tracker method:

```rust
pub fn track_linear_pricing_sample_applied(
    &self,
    node: NodeId,
    slot: u64,
    producer: NodeId,
) {
    self.send(Event::LinearPricingSampleApplied { node, slot, producer });
}
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Add the two `RunSummary` fields**

In `sim-rs/sim-cli/src/metrics/collector.rs`, in the `pub struct RunSummary` declaration:

```rust
    /// Multi-node noise metric (M6). Number of distinct slots at
    /// which the representative node validated >= 2 RBs.
    #[serde(default)]
    pub slot_battles_count: u64,
    /// Multi-node noise metric (M6). Pricing samples applied to RBs
    /// that the representative's chain-selection later orphaned.
    /// Equals sum over slot-battle slots of (N_at_slot - 1).
    #[serde(default)]
    pub orphaned_pricing_samples: u64,
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Add the accumulator to the collector**

In `pub struct MetricsCollector { ... }`:

```rust
    /// (slot -> set of producers) at the representative node.
    /// Drained at `finalise` to compute slot_battles_count and
    /// orphaned_pricing_samples.
    pricing_sample_producers_by_slot: HashMap<u64, HashSet<NodeId>>,
```

Initialize in `MetricsCollector::new(...)`:

```rust
    pricing_sample_producers_by_slot: HashMap::new(),
```

- [ ] **Step 7: Handle the event in `ingest`**

Add to the existing `match event { ... }`:

```rust
    Event::LinearPricingSampleApplied { node, slot, producer } => {
        if self
            .representative_node
            .as_ref()
            .is_some_and(|r| r == node)
        {
            self.pricing_sample_producers_by_slot
                .entry(*slot)
                .or_default()
                .insert(producer.clone());
        }
    }
```

- [ ] **Step 8: Compute in `finalise`**

In `pub fn finalise(mut self) -> (Vec<TimeSeriesRow>, RunSummary)`, after computing the existing hash:

```rust
    let mut slot_battles_count: u64 = 0;
    let mut orphaned_pricing_samples: u64 = 0;
    for producers in self.pricing_sample_producers_by_slot.values() {
        if producers.len() >= 2 {
            slot_battles_count += 1;
            orphaned_pricing_samples += (producers.len() - 1) as u64;
        }
    }
```

Then in the `RunSummary { ... }` struct literal add:

```rust
    slot_battles_count,
    orphaned_pricing_samples,
```

```bash
cargo test --workspace
```

Expected: all green. Pre-M6 baseline: 132 tests; post-M6 baseline: 133 tests (+1 collector test from Step 2).

The M2/M3 scenario-isolated unit-test goldens (`m2_two_lane.rs`, `m3_actors.rs`) should still pass — those scenarios construct minimal setups with no slot battles, so `slot_battles_count` defaults to 0 in their environments and the pricing event-stream hashes (the things they pin) are unaffected by the new field on `RunSummary` (the goldens hash `TXIncluded` + `TXEvictedQuoteDrift` event encoding, not `RunSummary` serialization). If anything goes red here, stop and diagnose.

- [ ] **Step 14: Commit**

```bash
git add sim-rs/sim-core/src/events.rs sim-rs/sim-core/src/sim/linear_leios.rs \
        sim-rs/sim-cli/src/metrics/{collector,comparison,diagnostics}.rs
git commit -m "M6: fork-resolution metric (slot_battles_count, orphaned_pricing_samples)"
```

### Task A5: Flip default-topology in 16 suite YAMLs

**Files:** all of `sim-rs/parameters/phase-2-sweep/suites/phase-2-*.yaml`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Confirm all 16 currently point at single-producer**

```bash
cd sim-rs
grep -l 'topology-single-producer' parameters/phase-2-sweep/suites/*.yaml | wc -l
```

Expected: 16.

- [ ] **Step 2: Bulk replace**

```bash
cd sim-rs
sed -i 's|parameters/phase-2-sweep/topology-single-producer\.yaml|parameters/phase-2-sweep/topology-cip-realistic.yaml|g' \
    parameters/phase-2-sweep/suites/*.yaml
grep -l 'topology-cip-realistic' parameters/phase-2-sweep/suites/*.yaml | wc -l
```

Expected: 16.

- [ ] **Step 3: Spot-check one**

```bash
grep -A2 'default-topology' sim-rs/parameters/phase-2-sweep/suites/phase-2-eip1559-robustness.yaml
```

Expected:
```
default-topology: parameters/phase-2-sweep/topology-cip-realistic.yaml
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add sim-rs/parameters/phase-2-sweep/suites/*.yaml
git commit -m "M6: flip 16 suites to topology-cip-realistic"
```

### Task A6: Smoke-test one (job, seed)

Validates: topology parses, vote-threshold=450 actually gets satisfied (i.e. enough votes accumulate per EB), no panics, EBs endorse, fork-resolution metric fires non-trivially.

**Files:** read-only (`sim-rs/output/`).

- [ ] **Step 1: Reduce slots for fast iteration**

The full suite uses `default-slots: 2000`. For smoke test, run a smaller variant.

```bash
cd sim-rs
mkdir -p output/m6-smoke
cat > /tmp/m6-smoke.yaml <<EOF
suite-name: m6-smoke
output-dir: output/m6-smoke
seeds: [1]
default-slots: 100
default-topology: parameters/phase-2-sweep/topology-cip-realistic.yaml
default-protocol: parameters/phase-2-sweep/protocol-base.yaml
default-demand: parameters/phase-2-sweep/demand/paper_like_moderate.yaml
jobs:
  - name: baseline_flat_fee
    pricing: parameters/phase-2-sweep/pricing/baseline_flat_fee.yaml
EOF
time ./target/release/experiment-suite run /tmp/m6-smoke.yaml
```

Expected: completes in 30-180 s (much slower than single-producer; 600 nodes × propagation is the cost). No panics.

- [ ] **Step 2: Inspect outputs**

```bash
cat sim-rs/output/m6-smoke/jobs/baseline_flat_fee/seed-1/attempt-001/metrics_comparison.txt | head -40
```

Sanity checks:
- `txs_submitted > 0`, `txs_included > 0` (basic plumbing).
- `pricing_ticks > 0`.
- `multiplier_floor_breaches == 0`.
- `slot_battles_count > 0` — expected: with rb-prob=0.05 over 600 producers, slot battles fire ~5% of slots × P(two producers in same slot) = small but non-zero. Over 100 slots, expect at least 1-3 battles.
- `orphaned_pricing_samples >= slot_battles_count`.

If `slot_battles_count == 0` after 100 slots, increase to 500 slots and re-run; if still 0, something is wrong with the metric wiring — debug before proceeding.

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify EB endorsement still fires**

```bash
grep -E '(rb_endorsed|eb_endorsed|certified)' sim-rs/output/m6-smoke/jobs/baseline_flat_fee/seed-1/attempt-001/diagnostics.log | head -10
```

(Field names depend on what diagnostics surfaces — check what exists. Goal: confirm certified EBs > 0, indicating the 450-vote threshold is being met. If certified == 0, vote-probability calibration is wrong.)

- [ ] **Step 4: Save smoke output for reference**

```bash
mv sim-rs/output/m6-smoke sim-rs/output/m6-smoke-baseline
```

(Keep around so later checks can compare against it.)

### Task A7: Code review checkpoint

**REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** superpowers:requesting-code-review.

Per the task spec: "Use superpowers:requesting-code-review after the implementation is functional but before regenerating goldens."

- [ ] **Step 1: Invoke the code review skill**

Brief the reviewer with: scope = M6 (topology + vote params + fork metric + suite flip); the implementation plan = `docs/phase-2/m6-implementation-plan.md`; verify-before-completion against the Phase A done criteria below.

- [ ] **Step 2: Address feedback**

Apply changes per reviewer feedback. Re-run `cargo test --workspace`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit any review fixes**

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "M6: review fixes"
```

### Phase A done criteria

- `cargo test --workspace` → green (133 tests, M2/M3 goldens still pinned).
- `topology-cip-realistic.yaml` exists, 600 nodes, sanity checks pass.
- 16 suite YAMLs point at the new topology.
- 4 protocol YAMLs have `vote-probability: 600`, `vote-threshold: 450`.
- Smoke-test run completes; metric is non-zero; EBs endorse.
- Code review applied.

**Stop here.** Do not regenerate goldens before user runs the full sweep — the goldens are tied to the simulator's deterministic event stream, and we want one regeneration pass aligned with the user's full-suite run.

- [ ] **Step 2: Update the doc-comment**

Replace the existing header doc-comment with:

```bash
# Run the phase-2 welfare suites in parallel.
#
# Usage:
#   scripts/run-parallel-suites.sh [N] [SUITE ...]
#
# N defaults to the number of CPUs. SUITE list defaults to all 16
# phase-2 suites (post-M6 multi-node).
#
# Each suite runs in its own process; jobs within a suite still run
# sequentially. Effective parallelism is capped at min(N, len(SUITE)).
#
# Multi-node (CIP-0164 topology, 600 pools) is roughly 30x slower per
# run than the M5-era single-producer baseline. Per-suite wall time
# ranges from ~5 min (eip1559-smoothing) to ~30 min (priority-only-*).
# Expect 1-3 hours total at -P 10 on the full 16-suite batch.
#
# Invokes `experiment-suite run`, which is resumable — partial output
# from a prior run is preserved and only the missing (job, seed) pairs
# are executed.
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add sim-rs/scripts/run-parallel-suites.sh
git commit -m "M6: default run-parallel-suites.sh to all 16 suites"
```

```
Phase A done. Smoke test passes; fork-resolution metric fires.

To kick off the full multi-node sweep:

  cd sim-rs && cargo build --release --bin experiment-suite
  cd sim-rs && bash scripts/run-parallel-suites.sh 10

Runtime: 1-3 h at -P 10. The script is resumable; if a suite gets
interrupted, re-running will skip Completed (job, seed) pairs.

When all 16 suites have output dirs under sim-rs/output/phase-2/*,
ping me and I'll resume with the variance experiment + goldens regen
+ write-up.
```

## Phase C — Post-compute (after user reports done)

### Task C1: Variance experiment

**Tuples to re-run (3, with 10 seeds each)**:
1. `phase-2-moderate-singlelane`, job `baseline_flat_fee`, demand `paper_like_moderate`
2. `phase-2-priority-only-unreserved`, job `multiplier_x4`, demand `paper_like_congested`
3. `phase-2-two-lane-both-dynamic`, job `unreserved_x4`, demand `sundaeswap_moderate`

(Adjust job/demand names after-the-fact to whatever's in each suite YAML.)

- [ ] **Step 1: Author a one-off variance suite YAML per tuple**

For each tuple, create `/tmp/m6-variance-<n>.yaml` with `seeds: [101, 102, ..., 110]` and the corresponding job/protocol/demand/topology config.

- [ ] **Step 2: Run each via `experiment-suite run`**

```bash
cd sim-rs
for f in /tmp/m6-variance-*.yaml; do
    ./target/release/experiment-suite run "$f"
done
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Compute welfare std-dev / mean**

A small Python script (one-shot, can be inline):

```bash
cd sim-rs
python3 - <<'EOF'
import json, os, statistics, glob
for tdir in sorted(glob.glob("output/m6-variance-*/jobs/*")):
    welfares = []
    for f in sorted(glob.glob(f"{tdir}/seed-*/attempt-001/run_summary.json")):
        with open(f) as fh:
            s = json.load(fh)
        # Pick the welfare proxy: priority_retained_value_total + standard_retained_value_total.
        welfares.append(s["priority_retained_value_total"] + s["standard_retained_value_total"])
    if welfares:
        mean = statistics.mean(welfares)
        sd = statistics.stdev(welfares) if len(welfares) > 1 else 0.0
        print(f"{tdir}: n={len(welfares)} mean={mean:.4f} sd={sd:.4f} cv={(sd/mean*100 if mean else 0):.3f}%")
EOF
```

Expected: coefficient of variation (CV) < 1 % per tuple per the task's threshold.

- [ ] **Step 4: Decision branch**

- If all 3 CVs < 1 %: proceed to Task C2. Record the numbers; they go into m6-handoff.md.
- If any CV ≥ 1 %: stop. Surface to user with the numbers; do NOT proceed with goldens regen or the headline welfare comparison.

### Task C2: Regenerate goldens

- [ ] **Step 1: Run with `UPDATE_GOLDENS=1`**

```bash
cd sim-rs
UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 cargo test --release -- --ignored determinism
```

Expected: 7 tests pass; the 7 `.goldens/<suite>.sha256` files are overwritten.

- [ ] **Step 2: Inspect diffs**

```bash
git diff sim-rs/parameters/phase-2-sweep/suites/.goldens/
```

Every hash should differ from the pre-M6 value (multi-node ⇒ different event stream).

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify by re-asserting**

```bash
cd sim-rs
cargo test --release -- --ignored determinism
```

Expected: 7 pass (now asserting against the freshly-written values).

### Task C3: `experiment-suite verify` on every suite

- [ ] **Step 1: Loop over all 16**

```bash
cd sim-rs
for suite in parameters/phase-2-sweep/suites/phase-2-*.yaml; do
    echo "=== $(basename "$suite") ==="
    ./target/release/experiment-suite verify "$suite" 2>&1 | tail -3
done
```

Expected: every suite prints `determinism verify ok` for every (job, seed) it iterates.

- [ ] **Step 2: If any drift**

Investigate the specific (job, seed) pair. Re-run, compare hashes, diagnose. Do NOT regenerate goldens to "fix" drift — drift indicates a bug.

### Task C4: Commit goldens

- [ ] **Step 1: Commit**

```bash
git add sim-rs/parameters/phase-2-sweep/suites/.goldens/
git commit -m "M6: regenerate suite goldens for multi-node topology"
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Annotated tag**

```bash
git tag -a m6-multi-node-goldens -m "Phase-2 suite goldens after M6 multi-node migration. Variance bound: <cv1>%/<cv2>%/<cv3>% (paper_like_moderate / paper_like_congested / sundaeswap_moderate)."
```

## Phase D — Documentation

### Task D1: m6-handoff.md

**Files:**
- Create: `docs/phase-2/m6-handoff.md`

**Sections (concrete content, no placeholders):**

```markdown
# M6 → multi-node migration handoff

## What M6 delivered
- 600-pool CIP-0164 topology (topology-cip-realistic.yaml + generator).
- vote-probability = 600, vote-threshold = 450 (75 % of n).
- Fork-resolution metric on RunSummary (slot_battles_count, orphaned_pricing_samples).
- 16 suite YAMLs flipped to multi-node.
- Suite goldens regenerated, tagged m6-multi-node-goldens.

## Slot-battle decision (no rollback path)
M1's known limitation — pricing state has no rollback on fork
resolution — carried forward. Multi-node + rb-prob=0.05 over 600
producers means slot battles occur frequently (rate measured at
<X%/slot> via the new metric). The alternatives are:
1. Build snapshot-and-replay rollback (significant work; the
   controller is a closure over u128 accumulators that don't
   currently serialize cleanly).
2. Accept the noise; quantify it via re-run variance; document
   the bound.

We chose (2). The variance experiment ran each of three
representative (job, seed) tuples 10x with different seeds and
measured welfare coefficient of variation:
- paper_like_moderate × baseline_flat_fee: CV = <x.xx>%
- paper_like_congested × priority_only_unreserved_x4: CV = <x.xx>%
- sundaeswap_moderate × two_lane_both_dynamic_unreserved_x4: CV = <x.xx>%
All under the 1 % threshold ⇒ slot-battle noise is bounded and
the headline welfare comparison stands.

## Architectural changes
- `Event::LinearPricingSampleApplied` (new variant).
- `EventTracker::track_linear_pricing_sample_applied` (new method).
- `RunSummary.slot_battles_count`, `RunSummary.orphaned_pricing_samples` (new fields).
- One-line emission in `linear_leios.rs::publish_rb` before `apply_priced_block`.

## Known limitations carried forward
- Same M1 rollback limitation, now quantified.
- Cross-arch determinism still intra-arch only.
- Representative-node metric still picks the lexicographically
  smallest pool — for multi-node this is `pool-000` (largest stake).
  Per-node welfare divergence is not reported.

## Gotchas
- `topology-cip-realistic.yaml` is generated; never hand-edit. Re-run
  `sim-rs/scripts/generate-cip-topology.py` to regenerate.
- Per-RB cost is ~30x single-producer. Full 16-suite sweep ~1-3 h at -P 10.
- The smallest stake pool needs `stake × 0.05 ≥ 100` for VRF
  quantization; generator asserts this.

## Forward pointers
- Welfare numbers: docs/phase-2/multi-node-results.md.
- Per-suite hashes: parameters/phase-2-sweep/suites/.goldens/.
```

Fill in `<X>` / `<x.xx>` from Phase C results.

(Use the template above; fill in actuals.)

```bash

git commit -m "M6: handoff doc"
```

## Self-review checklist (run before handoff)

- [ ] Every task has a file path. No "TBD" file references.
- [ ] Every code step has actual code, not pseudocode.
- [ ] Task A4 names the same struct/field/event throughout (`LinearPricingSampleApplied`, `slot_battles_count`, `orphaned_pricing_samples`, `pricing_sample_producers_by_slot`).
- [ ] Phase B's handoff command matches the actual script default path.
- [ ] Phase C's variance threshold (1 % CV) matches the task spec's stated bound.
- [ ] All 16 suite YAMLs are accounted for (A5 commits all of them, B1's script defaults reference all of them).
- [ ] No goldens regen happens before Phase C (no `UPDATE_GOLDENS=1` step in Phase A).
- [ ] Code review (A7) gates goldens regen (C2).
